Crazy watching this now after you passed away. When I heard the news I immediately thought of this video which for most of my life I thought was the best video on the platform. I was 13 when this video came out and I remember going to school and telling all my friends how this Minecraft guy I was subscribed to (only 4k subs) just killed all these super famous people. I then found out AidenRK, Danel1515, Epreseley, Kastrealm, and AskFox weren't famous people to anyone else other than me; but in my eyes Techno was the biggest celebrity on the planet because of this video. I played it day after day and Frame of Mind was already my favorite song at the time even before the video so it never got old. I'm 21 years old now and haven't played in a few years but when I say this video will never be topped I don't say it lightly. I just remember how it made me feel when it came out and absolutely blew my mind. Nothing will ever come close, thanks for the memories and goodbye Techno. You really couldn't have done any better.

I miss this game so much. It was really the only multiplayer video game I was truly excited to play every day. I miss the summer 2015 grind I did for armorer VIII (that was pretty lame for most then but not to me) I miss the rage, the excitement, the thrills, watching techno's daily uploads. This game really meant something to me. I logged onto blitz a few months ago and all I can say is ;-;. It wasn't to last because of the imbalance of noobs and experienced players. I've moved on with life, but I wouldn't mind having one of those grind days back.

Coming back to this one tonight. Still proud of the fight I put up, I think I would've had you if DM didn't begin for a couple more seconds. Thanks for all the memories, this video being just one of the dozens and our first encounter which led to me becoming obsessed with beating you for a while, little sister even wrote in one of my birthday cards from close to a decade ago now that I'd get that win eventually. Took me cleaning you up on Citadel though, I never had a chance in a fair fight. My childhood is so hazy yet I look back upon these moments in time with nothing but clarity. You will be sorely missed by the Blitz community, old and new alike. The impact you had on the game, and the server as a whole, cannot be understated. You were a true gem of a human being, and it pains me to know that you'll never read this. Wish we'd talked more, but to get two socially anxious people to communicate by will you'd likely have to lock them in a room together. 

From 100 subs to 10 million, you really did it all. I love you and will continue to hold these memories close to my heart, rest easy buddy.
You left a void we can never fill. Thanks for all the memories.

He felt someone else with him too.  Someone else within his thoughts.  Strange  - but it didn’t frighten him. Though he never felt it there before, it was like he realized the presence had been there through his whole life, watching everything unfold, mostly without much interest but missing nothing. Its perceptions were drastically different to his… able to observe the rise and fall of individuals… races… lineages… the world itself in just seconds if it chose. For a brief, fleeting moment, he could see what it saw. A story he couldn’t understand flashed within his mind’s eye. Images of a small bird… a little fish… things he could not recognize… so many lifetimes of so many things. He saw behind and ahead all at once, and he saw others already gone and others unborn… And then it spoke.



You did well.



And then nothing. Not blackness, not darkness, not silence. The absolute lack of anything at all, something no mind can truly understand because it only occurs when consciousness itself ceases to exist. As quickly as the presence had made itself known, and before he could understand any of what he was seeing, it was all over.

It is actually a metaphor for life and the start and fall of a toxic friendgroup.
In the beginning they cant and wont hurt eachother by any means.
Then one of them begins to push the others out of the friend group with bullying. (Explosives is a metaphor for bullying and harrasment.)
Now there are only 2 left in the friendgroup left, but the toxic friend begins to start rumours about the other friend to make him look better.
We can see that because we know the explosions means bullying and harrasment, and the toxic friend starts placing more people to bully that other friend.
The toxic friend also drags other mindless people into the toxicity. (Portrayed by the pigs.)
 Until finally the whole thing explodes and the toxic friend is all alone.

I was max loot and sharp 1 stone sword on winter against a horsetamer, a wolf 7 and a no armor non donor. The non donor got the blitz star and used holywarrior killing the wolftamer. I run over to said non donor thinking Holywarrior had run out. But it still had about 20 seconds of lighting left so I basically ran myself into death itself. Instead of going out like a little bitch I decided to fight and go out like a badass. I started to jitter click (a tactic that I don't often use especially back at this time) and somehow I managed to take wood sword and lighting in the face and still manage to kill this person who had regen 4. I lived with 0.5 hearts and I was speachless. This game I was using Baker 6 so in DM I splashed resistance and won easy. I feel so honored to be the first comment. All of my hardwork and dedication has paid off. Getting the top comment has been a dream of mine for many years, and i would like to thank those who have helped me along the way. First and foremost i would like to thank god for giving me this opportunity. Next i would like to thank my parents. I want to thank my friend Christian Green, for being really skinny and always there for me. I would also like to thank my pet tadpole for surviving against all odds for over a week. Next i would like to thank the squirrel that lives in my backyard for climbing trees because that gives me inspiration that i need to get through the day. This is a special moment in my life and i would like to thank any of my unmentioned friends and family that have helped me along the way. This moment will be a moment that i will never forget. I just remmembered a few other people i would like to thank; youtube, the fish i caught in the third grade, my light in my room bc i wouldn’t be able to see the keyboard without it, the internet for letting me go on youtube, my house because without it i would be homeless, and last but not least i would like to thank all the people out there that actually took time out of their day to read this. I cannot stress how much of a big deal to me this is. I have been trying to be the first comment on a post for years, but that has not been possible until this amazing day. Hopefully my good luck will continue, but this is undoubtedly a rare occasion. If you asked me how i did this, i would say, you can achieve anything u set your mind on. To all the kids out there reading this, i would like to tell them to follow their dreams. Being the top comment is amazing, thank you everyone.

He felt someone else with him too.  Someone else within his thoughts.  Strange  - but it didn’t frighten him. Though he never felt it there before, it was like he realized the presence had been there through his whole life, watching everything unfold, mostly without much interest but missing nothing. Its perceptions were drastically different to his… able to observe the rise and fall of individuals… races… lineages… the world itself in just seconds if it chose. For a brief, fleeting moment, he could see what it saw. A story he couldn’t understand flashed within his mind’s eye. Images of a small bird… a little fish… things he could not recognize… so many lifetimes of so many things. He saw behind and ahead all at once, and he saw others already gone and others unborn… And then it spoke.



You did well.



And then nothing. Not blackness, not darkness, not silence. The absolute lack of anything at all, something no mind can truly understand because it only occurs when consciousness itself ceases to exist. As quickly as the presence had made itself known, and before he could understand any of what he was seeing, it was all over.

Welcome, to those in the future. Earlier today, on June 30th, Technoblade's final video was released. One of the few-if not, the only-without Technoblade in it. This video was narrated by the father of Alex(Technoblade's real name). After a long and hard fight with cancer, having doctors steal his leg muscles, losing one singular bone, and farming plenty of potatoes, he was gone.

Just half an hour ago I watched his second most recent video and I just heard a 22-year-old man say "fishie" while pointing at things like a small child.
Technoblade was a treasure to us all. I'll miss his college stories, I'll miss his life updates, I'll miss his stupid jokes, I'll miss him shooting people into the air like a volleyball, I'll miss him bullying orphans. . . I'll miss Alex.

Technoblade never dies. . .

Missing you already techno, rest easy king. You'll be missed by millions, and you'll leave a legacy of greatness behind. In our heart's you'll never die. For anyone in the future, Techno's death was announced earlier today, on June 30th 2022, in a video by his father. This is, at the time, his lowest viewed video, with 2,199 views. Besides that, I'll miss you and all the laughs and memories you've given me. Rest in Peace to the greatest minecraft player who ever lived who was also a great sibling and son.

Goodbye Technoblade

That's the first video I've watched from techno, and why I subbed to him when he was just with a few hundred subs.
I've wanted to watch this video again, remembering you, and Aiden, and all the nostalgic memories; I will, and always, miss you.

I was going into 8th grade when I watched and commented on this video and now I'm going into college. Thank you Techno for all the laughs, for influencing me to play skywars, bedwars and subsequently meet my best friend. I don't think I could ever forget you, and I'm glad that the merch I bought is going into helping your siblings do what inspires and motivates them. I genuinely hope they find happiness, and find peace in your death, as I know it may never get better but it can and will get easier. 

If you're reading this, aforementioned siblings, please know that your brother was a great person who helped so so many people. When I started watching his videos I was still dealing with the aftermath of my father committing suicide, and having someone to look up to and find joy in, even trying to be like them, helped tremendously. If there's any advice I can give, it's that you can be angry. You have the right to be angry at whoever it is you think is at fault, however, it doesn't deny the fact that this was never something in your control. You may wish you spent more time with him, or never had that fight, or was just overall there for them more, but you did what you could, and he knows that. It was never, ever your fault. In the end, he wouldn't have cared about what you did or could have done wrong, he was just happy that you were there. There to talk to, there to have as a family, there to love, you being alive is all he could ask for. I don't believe in god, but rest assured that he's still here with you, and waiting patiently for you to come and join him when the time comes. He loves you just as much as you love him. If I ever died I would at least want my younger siblings to know that. 
I hope your nights are calm, and that the ache you feel now eases up with time. Sleep well, and live knowing that your memory of him will never fade. Keep his cologne if he had any, mark down the brand, and when you miss him spray some on a shirt of his. Keep pictures in places only you will know to look. Don't ever forget all the fun you had. You will cry, you will scream, maybe even feel like you yourself want to go with him, but now isn't the time. Keep your chin high and live for each other, for your parents, for your dog. It won't be easy, it will never be easy, but it will get easier. I wish you the best of luck, I will never know your exact situation, but I hope that this helps.

I just miss the small things, like the way he said "amazin," and his laugh that I always found to be contagious. These individual traits, along with his sense of humor and supernatural gift with the game, created such an interesting style of content. His wholesome demeanor, which seems to be present in different forms throughout his entire career, seemed to make me as a viewer feel like I was part of something special when I watched him. Even now when I watch his videos I seem to find moments where I can just let his death leave my mind, and it is almost like I am watching his content for the first time again. That is what his community means when they say he can't die. I'm unsure if I really put my thoughts into words properly, but I felt that I had to try and express the emotions that arise when I view his content. Despite my own insecurities there are a couple of things that I know for sure. 
This is what made him the king that he was, 
This is what made him Technoblade,

and finally if you haven't heard or seen it enough, this is why
TECHNOBLADE  NEVER DIES!!!
